RESEARCH2 RECONCILIATION (CR7): After ModifyStep completes all fixes, iterate through research_context.quality_findings (from Research2). For each finding that was addressed by a ModifyStep fix, update finding status: { original_severity: RED|YELLOW, status: 'fixed_in_modifystep', fix_description: string, fix_step: string }. Store as pipeline_metadata.research2_reconciled_findings. Pass to Generate2 (ThreeDimTest) so it can perform verification re-test on fixed items rather than independent re-discovery. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='ModifyStep', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'ModifyStep', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass modified_file_path to Generate2.
ModifyStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Modify applies quality improvements based on Research2 findings.
ModifyStep.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip quality improvement -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

Generate2.step1: [ASSERT] ModifyStep executed. If false → go back to ModifyStep. | INPUT VALIDATION: verify modified_file_path from ModifyStep is non-null and file exists. If null → fallback: use draft_path from Generate1 and log warning in pipeline_metadata. Delegate to generate.aisop.json — pass modified_file_path, workspace_dir, quality_threshold.
Generate2.step2: Collect draft_path, test_report, traffic_report, weighted_score. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { generate2_weighted_score: weighted_score }. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='Generate2', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'Generate2', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass test_report and weighted_score to QualityGate.
Generate2.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. This is the quality retest pass — runs ThreeDimTest on modified draft to verify quality improvements. NOTE: ThreeDimTest now actively generates sub_mermaid decomposition proposals (C5-SUB_MERMAID-DECOMPOSE) when node count >= 16 and feasible sub-graphs are detected. If Generate2 produces a RED_WITH_FIX result with sub_mermaid_proposal, the pipeline routes through PauseHuman for user approval before ApplyFixes.step1_5 executes the decomposition.
Generate2.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: use Generate1 draft with warning -> inform. SEVERITY: CRITICAL (per PL27) — max 3 retries then abort.

QualityGate.step1: [ASSERT] Generate2 executed. If false → go back to Generate2. | Evaluate weighted_score from test_report (C*0.25 + I*0.45 + D*0.30). Threshold: strict>=3.6, standard>=2.7, relaxed>=2.0.
QualityGate.step2: PASS (weighted_score >= threshold) -> Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { quality_gate_result: 'PASS', quality_gate_retries: retry_count }. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'QualityGate', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. MID-PIPELINE CHAIN CHECK: invoke attestation_chain_validator at this checkpoint. Proceed to Research3. EVENT EMISSION (per STANDARD.PL20 event_hooks): if pipeline_context.event_hooks configured, emit on_gate_pass event: { gate: 'QualityGate', result: 'PASS', weighted_score, timestamp }. TRUST LEVEL CHECK: if pipeline_task.effective_trust_level < AIAP.md trust_level.level, enforce minimum 'standard' quality_threshold regardless of configured threshold. Log trust downgrade enforcement. AUTO-DOWNGRADE TRIGGER: if test_report shows I2 security dimension score < dimension_floor (indicating security failure), automatically downgrade pipeline_task.effective_trust_level by 1 (minimum T1). Record { event: 'auto_downgrade', trigger: 'I2_security_floor_violation', old_level, new_level, stage: 'QualityGate' } in pipeline_metadata.trust_downgrade_events. This ensures security failures automatically restrict subsequent pipeline stages.
QualityGate.step3: FAIL -> classify failure_category from test_report: identify lowest-scoring dimension (cognitive/intrinsic/detail). Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { quality_gate_result: 'FAIL', quality_gate_failure_category: lowest_dim }. Route to ModifyStep with failure_category hint for targeted dimension-specific fixes. Track retry_count. EVENT EMISSION: if event_hooks configured, emit on_gate_fail event: { gate: 'QualityGate', result: 'FAIL', failure_category: lowest_dim, weighted_score, retry_count, timestamp }.
QualityGate.step4: DIMENSION FLOOR: if any individual dimension score (C, I, or D) falls below dimension_floor (strict: 3/B, standard: 2/C, relaxed: 1/D), classify as FAIL regardless of weighted_score — this prevents high scores in one dimension from masking critical failures in another. Priority: Intrinsic floor violations take precedence (I2 security is non-negotiable). When FAIL due to dimension_floor, set failure_category to the floored dimension and route to ModifyStep with targeted fix directive.
QualityGate.step5: GLOBAL RETRY BUDGET: init 3 in PipelineStart. Each ModifyStep passage(any gate)→decrement. 0→only FORCE_PASS(+quality_warning) or ABORT. Track global_retry_count. QRG1 REGRESSION(Evolve/Modify): delta=weighted_score-old_weighted_score. <-0.1→WARNING, <-0.3→ERROR+ModifyStep regression-fix. >=-0.1→log no regression. DENOMINATOR NORMALIZATION: if applicable items differ old→new (I/C/D dimensions), normalize old scores before delta comparison: (I) new_applicable_I_items≠old→recompute old I_avg with new items scored at existing mean, (C) node_count changed→normalize C5 fractal with new denominator, (D) module_count changed→normalize MF cross-module with new denominator. Use all 3 normalized dimensions for normalized_old_weighted_score. Log {dimension_changed, old/new_denominator, normalized_score}. Skip Create mode.
QualityGate.step5_5: DIMENSION_DELTA_ANALYSIS (B5 v1.8.0 — Resolve.ai 2026 root cause analysis with multi-telemetry correlation; LakeFS 2026 data quality metrics dimension tracking; DeCaf ML-based automated regression diagnosis): When weighted_score delta < 0 (score decreased from previous version), automatically diagnose which dimension(s) caused the decline and classify the root cause. PURPOSE: enable informed rollback/continue decisions by providing actionable diagnosis instead of bare delta numbers. METHOD: (1) DIMENSION DELTAS: compute per-dimension delta: delta_C = current_C - baseline_C, delta_I = current_I - baseline_I, delta_D = current_D - baseline_D. Compute weighted contribution of each delta: contrib_C = delta_C * 0.25, contrib_I = delta_I * 0.45, contrib_D = delta_D * 0.30. (2) PRIMARY CONTRIBUTOR: identify dimension with largest negative weighted contribution as primary_contributor. If all deltas >= 0 but weighted delta < 0 due to rounding → classify as ROUNDING_ARTIFACT. (3) ROOT CAUSE CLASSIFICATION: (a) COMPLEXITY_GROWTH — primary_contributor is C AND (node_count increased OR new modules added OR new sub_mermaid added). Evidence: 'C dimension dropped due to increased structural complexity.' (b) SCORING_METHODOLOGY_CHANGE — primary_contributor's applicable_count changed between versions (e.g., I items N/A→applicable or vice versa). Evidence: 'I dimension score changed due to applicable item count shift ({old_count}→{new_count}).' (c) GENUINE_REGRESSION — primary_contributor dropped AND no structural/methodology change detected. Evidence: 'D dimension dropped on {check_id} — indicates potential functional regression.' (d) THRESHOLD_SHIFT — scoring thresholds or weights changed between versions. Evidence: 'Score change caused by threshold/weight configuration change, not content change.' (4) OUTPUT: dimension_delta_report = { overall_delta: float, primary_contributor: 'C'|'I'|'D'|'ROUNDING', root_cause_type: 'COMPLEXITY_GROWTH'|'SCORING_METHODOLOGY_CHANGE'|'GENUINE_REGRESSION'|'THRESHOLD_SHIFT'|'ROUNDING_ARTIFACT', per_dimension: { C: { prev: float, curr: float, delta: float, weighted_contrib: float }, I: { prev: float, curr: float, delta: float, weighted_contrib: float }, D: { prev: float, curr: float, delta: float, weighted_contrib: float } }, evidence: string }. (5) INTEGRATION: pass dimension_delta_report to ReviewStep Rollback Decision Engine as additional input. GENUINE_REGRESSION with delta < -0.15 → adds +1 to rollback high_count. COMPLEXITY_GROWTH and SCORING_METHODOLOGY_CHANGE → informational only, do not trigger rollback. (6) DISPLAY: when delta < 0, present: 'QUALITY DELTA ANALYSIS: {overall_delta:+.3f} — Primary: {primary_contributor} ({root_cause_type}). {evidence}.' SKIP: Create mode (no baseline), or delta >= 0 (no decline to diagnose).
QualityGate.step6: QRG3 ABSOLUTE FLOOR CHECK (ALL pipeline operations including Create): if weighted_score < 3.0, classify as FAIL regardless of threshold. Record pipeline_metadata.qrg3_triggered = true. Emit ERROR: 'QRG3 absolute quality floor breached (weighted_score < 3.0).' This check has no mode exemption — applies to Create, Evolve, and Modify pipelines per STANDARD.performance.quality_regression.QRG3. QRG-CREATE (CR12 v1.2.0, Create mode only): if old_version_context is null (Create mode), apply CREATE-specific quality gates as substitute for QRG1 regression check: (1) INITIAL QUALITY FLOOR: weighted_score >= 4.0 (Grade A minimum). If below: FAIL with 'QRG-CREATE: New program must achieve Grade A or higher (weighted_score >= 4.0). Current: {score}.' (2) SECURITY FLOOR: I2 score >= 4. If below: FAIL with 'QRG-CREATE: New program must have robust security guards (I2 >= 4). Current: {I2_score}.' (3) MODULE BALANCE: for Pattern B+ programs, compute max_module_nodes / min_module_nodes ratio. If ratio > 3.0: YELLOW with 'QRG-CREATE: Module size imbalance detected (ratio {ratio}). Consider redistributing functionality.' (4) INTENT COVERAGE: verify each NLU intent has at least 1 simulation scenario. If any intent uncovered: YELLOW with 'QRG-CREATE: Intent {intent} has no simulation scenario.' Record pipeline_metadata.qrg_create_results = { initial_quality, security_floor, module_balance, intent_coverage }. SKIP: for Evolve/Modify modes (use QRG1-QRG5 instead).
QualityGate.constraints: Max 2 retries then force pass with WARNING: pipeline_metadata.quality_gate_result = 'FORCE_PASS'. EVENT EMISSION: if event_hooks configured, emit on_gate_warn event: { gate: 'QualityGate', result: 'FORCE_PASS', weighted_score, retry_count, timestamp }. test_report null -> skip to Research3 with disclaimer. AUTO-FIX DELEGATION: if quality_gate_result='FAIL' and retry_count < max_retries, before routing to ModifyStep, check if issues are auto-fixable per STANDARD.data_contracts.auto_fix_proposal categories (format/consistency/metadata/reference). If all failing issues are auto-fixable with confidence>=MEDIUM, delegate to review.AutoFixEngine instead of ModifyStep. Record auto_fix_attempted=true in pipeline_metadata.
QualityGate.Error: Unparseable -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip to Research3 with WARNING, quality_gate_result='SKIP' -> inform. SEVERITY: CRITICAL (per PL27) — max 3 retries then abort.

Research3.step1: [ASSERT] QualityGate executed. If false → go back to QualityGate. | CACHE CHECK (per STANDARD.PL17): compute cache_key = SHA-256(target_content_hash + 'compliance' + workspace_dir). Look up .version_history/.cache/{cache_key}.json. If found AND age < TTL (from pipeline_context.cache_policy or base TTL) AND cache_bypass=false: load cached research_context, record cache_hit in pipeline_metadata.cache_hits, skip delegation. Else: record 'Research3' in pipeline_metadata.cache_misses. Delegate to research.aisop.json — pass topic (original user topic from pipeline context), workspace_dir, research_mode='compliance'. On success: write result to cache.
Research3.step2: Collect research_context (with research_mode='compliance'). Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { research3_verified: research_context.verified_count }. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='Research3', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'Research3', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass research_context to ValidateStep.
Research3.constraints: ALWAYS delegate (unless cache hit). Focuses on regulatory compliance (GDPR/HIPAA/PCI-DSS/CCPA/EU AI Act), formal threat assessment (AT1-AT6), and standards citations (NIST/ISO). Excludes general implementation quality patterns (those belong to Research2). Output is ALWAYS named research_context per STANDARD.PL7.
Research3.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: use NIST/EU AI Act general references -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ValidateStep.step1: [ASSERT] Research3 executed. If false → go back to Research3. | STAGE INPUT VALIDATION: verify draft_path from Generate2 exists and is valid JSON via file_system. If invalid → skip validation, log warning in pipeline_metadata, pass empty validation_report to SimulateStep. Read draft .aisop.json via file_system. INPUT VALIDATION: (A) Type guard — string path only. (B) Size guard — reject >500KB. (C) Injection guard — strip shell metacharacters. (D) Path guard — reject '..', validate within workspace_dir. (E) Encoding guard — NFC normalize. Reject nesting >20. Execute validation checklist: STRUCTURAL (1-6) per Validate.step2, INTEGRITY (7-10) per Validate.step3, QUALITY (11-14) per Validate.step4. Additionally run COMPLIANCE (15+): if research_context.compliance_notes from Research3 provided, for each applicable requirement verify disclosure/disclaimer/safeguard language and I2/I3 guards. Score as COMPLIANT/GAP/PARTIAL. LICENSE COMPLIANCE CHECK: read target AIAP.md license field. Validate: (a) if field present, value must be valid SPDX identifier or 'proprietary' (per STANDARD license_spec). (b) if value matches protocol version pattern 'AIAP V*', flag as LICENSE_SEMANTIC_ERROR — license should be an SPDX identifier, not a protocol version. (c) if license='proprietary', verify terms_url or contact field present. (d) if target program intends aiap-store publication (check AIAP.md for store-related fields), license field is MANDATORY (PL25). Score: COMPLIANT/GAP/PARTIAL. Include in validation_result.
ValidateStep.step2: Compile validation_report per STANDARD.data_contracts.validation_report: { pass_count, total_checks, issues: [{id, severity, description}] }. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { validate_pass, validate_total }. PATTERN E IMPLICIT DEPENDENCY CHECK: if target program uses Pattern E (memory/ directory), scan all module .aisop.json files for file_system operations referencing memory/ paths. Build implicit_dependency_edges: for each file written by module A in memory/ and read by module B, add edge A→B. If any module with memory/ writes was modified in this pipeline run, flag all dependent readers as 'implicit dependency changed — verify data contract compatibility'. Report as additional MF check item. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='ValidateStep', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'ValidateStep', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass validation_report to SimulateStep.
ValidateStep.step3: VERSION SYNC CHECK: read the 'version' field from all .aisop.json files in the target program AND the version field from AIAP.md. All N+1 values (N modules + 1 AIAP.md) must be identical. Any mismatch → FAIL with detailed discrepancy list: [{file, declared_version, expected_version}]. This check runs after all modifications and before governance_hash computation. EXTENDED VERSION SYNC (MF29 v3.4.0): Beyond .aisop.json version consistency, also verify: (1) AIAP.md version field matches. (2) AIAP.md module node counts match actual .aisop.json function counts. (3) AIAP.md module side_effects consistent with tool declarations. (4) quality_baseline.json version field matches (if exists) — YELLOW if mismatch. (5) agent_card.json version field matches (if exists) — YELLOW if mismatch. (6) AIAP.md quality.weighted_score matches quality_baseline.weighted_score — YELLOW if mismatch (MF30). Full metadata sync coverage: AIAP.md + all .aisop.json + agent_card.json + quality_baseline.json. MF31 MERMAID-FUNCTION CONSISTENCY (P4 v3.5.0): parse aisop.main Mermaid graph→extract all rectangle nodes (non-diamond, non-Start, non-End/endNode). For each rectangle node name, verify a matching key exists in functions{}. Missing function→YELLOW+AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: 'MF31: Mermaid node {name} has no function — remove from graph.' Reverse check: for each functions{} key, verify it appears as a node in Mermaid. Missing node→YELLOW+AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: 'MF31: function {name} not in Mermaid — add to graph.' Diamond nodes ({...?}) exempt — decisions within parent nodes. Exception: PascalCase diamonds (e.g., TypeGate, PostGate)→AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: rename to lowercase or merge into parent edges. MF31c ENDNODE NAMING (P6 v3.8.0, B5 v3.9.0 SUB-GRAPH COVERAGE): iterate over ALL aisop.* keys in each module (not just aisop.main). For each sub-graph: check terminal nodes. 'End', 'end', 'Finish', or ANY variant other than 'endNode((End))'→YELLOW+AUTO-FIX-CANDIDATE: 'MF31c: sub-graph {subgraph_key} terminal {name} should be endNode((End)).' This catches sub_mermaid sub-graphs (e.g., intent_dispatch using DispatchEnd((End))). MF31d FRACTAL_EXEMPT NODE COUNT (B7 v3.9.0): if module has fractal_exempt field, parse claimed node counts from text (regex: /(\d+)\s*(functional)?\s*nodes/i). Compare against actual Mermaid functional node count (total - Start - endNode). If claimed != actual → YELLOW: 'MF31d: fractal_exempt claims {claimed} nodes but Mermaid shows {actual}.' MF32 MODULE EXISTENCE VALIDATION (B1 v3.9.0): read agent_card.json modules[]. For each module_id, derive expected file name from last segment (e.g., 'soulbot_chat.insights' → look for file containing 'insights' in *.aisop.json listing). Scan target directory for all .aisop.json files via file_system. If any module_id has no corresponding file → RED: 'MF32: module {module_id} listed in agent_card.json but no matching .aisop.json file found on disk.' VERSION DRIFT CROSS-VALIDATION (B4 v3.9.0): if quality_baseline.json exists AND has evolution_history array with ≥1 entry: read evolution_history[-1].to (last evolution target version). Compare against AIAP.md version field. If mismatch → RED: 'VERSION DRIFT: quality_baseline evolution_history shows last version {history_ver} but AIAP.md declares {file_ver}. Files were not updated during prior evolutions.' This detects accumulated version drift from legacy Creator versions that didn't enforce version sync. All MF31/MF32 issues flagged as auto_fix_category='mermaid_consistency' for ReviewStep AUTO-FIX consumption.
ValidateStep.step3_5: MF33 CROSS-MODULE PARAMETER NAME DRIFT DETECTION (B1 v1.3.0 + B3 v1.4.0 CALIBRATION ENHANCEMENT — RapidFuzz normalized Levenshtein similarity; Jaro-Winkler prefix-sensitive matching; NAACL 2024 semantic drift detection): For Pattern B+ programs with 2+ modules, scan all delegation steps across all modules for parameter passing patterns. EXTRACTION: regex scan each step text for delegation patterns: 'delegate to {module} — pass {param_list}', 'pass {param} to {module}', '{module} with {param}={value}'. For each extracted delegation: (a) CALLER SIDE — extract parameter names from the delegation text. (b) CALLEE SIDE — read target module's parameters{} section, extract declared parameter names. (c) MATCH CHECK — for each caller-side param name, verify exact match exists in callee-side parameters{}. (d) ADAPTIVE FUZZY MATCH (B3 v1.4.0 — replaces fixed Levenshtein≤2): if no exact match, apply LENGTH-ADAPTIVE THRESHOLD: (i) SHORT PARAM GUARD — if param_name length ≤ 4 characters, require EXACT MATCH ONLY (research: edit distance 1 on a 4-char string = 25% difference, unacceptable false positive rate; e.g., 'id' matching 'ip' or 'mode' matching 'node'). (ii) MEDIUM PARAMS (5-12 chars) — threshold = max(2, floor(len(param_name) * 0.25)). Use Levenshtein distance. (iii) LONG PARAMS (13+ chars) — use NORMALIZED SIMILARITY: similarity = 1 - (levenshtein_distance / (len(caller_param) + len(callee_param))). Threshold: similarity ≥ 0.75. This prevents long parameter names from having disproportionately high absolute thresholds. (iv) SYNONYM TABLE — before flagging drift, check common parameter name synonyms: { 'context'↔'ctx', 'config'↔'cfg', 'configuration'↔'config', 'params'↔'parameters', 'dir'↔'directory', 'msg'↔'message', 'info'↔'information', 'req'↔'request', 'res'↔'response', 'resp'↔'response', 'err'↔'error', 'cb'↔'callback', 'fn'↔'function', 'val'↔'value', 'idx'↔'index', 'num'↔'number', 'cnt'↔'count' }. If caller_param and callee_param are synonyms → PASS (not drift). (v) PREFIX MATCH BONUS (Jaro-Winkler inspired) — if caller_param and callee_param share a common prefix ≥ 50% of the shorter name's length (e.g., 'engagement_score' vs 'engagement_metric'), reduce severity from YELLOW to ADVISORY: 'MF33-PARAM-NEAR: {caller_param} and {callee_param} share prefix "{common_prefix}" — likely intentional variant, verify.' (vi) If fuzzy match detected (not synonym, not prefix bonus): flag as YELLOW 'MF33-PARAM-DRIFT: caller passes "{caller_param}" to {module} but callee declares "{callee_param}" (similarity: {score:.2f}) — possible rename drift'. (e) MISSING PARAM — if no exact match, no fuzzy match, and no synonym: flag as YELLOW 'MF33-PARAM-MISSING: caller passes "{caller_param}" to {module} but callee has no matching parameter declaration'. Report: { delegations_scanned: int, exact_matches: int, synonym_matches: int, prefix_near_matches: int, drift_detected: [{ caller_module, callee_module, caller_param, callee_param, similarity_score, match_method }], missing_params: [{ caller_module, callee_module, param_name }] }. FLAG ACCUMULATION LIMIT: max 50 flags per program — if exceeded, report first 50 with summary 'N additional drift/missing flags truncated'. SKIP for single-module programs or Create mode with no inter-module delegations.
ValidateStep.constraints: Show fixes, not just failures. Validation issues -> flag for ReviewStep, do NOT loop back. Shares STRUCTURAL/INTEGRITY/QUALITY checklist with standalone Validate — single source of truth.
ValidateStep.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip validation with WARNING -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

SimulateStep.step1: [ASSERT] ValidateStep executed. If false → go back to ValidateStep. | INPUT VALIDATION: verify draft_path exists via file_system. If invalid → skip simulation, log warning in pipeline_metadata, pass empty simulation_report to ReviewStep. Delegate to simulate.aisop.json — pass target_path (draft_path), simulation_mode='full', workspace_dir, research_context (from Research3 — always named research_context per STANDARD.PL7; primarily contains compliance_notes for Category F domain scenarios), research_quality_context (from Research2 — best_practices for enriching domain-specific scenario generation; optional enhancement), evolution_context (from pipeline context for comparative scenario testing). BASELINE COMPARISON: if previous_baseline loaded by PipelineStart, pass to simulate for regression detection against stored quality scores and coverage.
SimulateStep.step2: Collect simulation_report (path coverage, dead nodes, domain scenario coverage). Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { simulate_path_coverage, simulate_scenario_coverage, simulate_dead_nodes }. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='SimulateStep', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. SIMULATION QUALITY INDICATOR: compute from simulation_report scenario results — RED: any scenario has severity=RED → simulate_quality_indicator='RED'. YELLOW: 0 red but 3+ yellow scenarios → simulate_quality_indicator='YELLOW'. GREEN: 0 red and <=2 yellow → simulate_quality_indicator='GREEN'. Store simulate_yellow_count, simulate_red_count, simulate_quality_indicator in pipeline_metadata. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'SimulateStep', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain.
SimulateStep.step3: POST-SIMULATE AUTO-FIX (optional): if simulate_quality_indicator is RED and simulate_red_count <= 3, offer targeted auto-fix cycle: (a) extract RED issue descriptions from simulation_report.issues with location info, (b) delegate to modify.aisop.json with targeted fix directives (user_request = 'Fix simulation RED issues: {issue_descriptions}', research_context from Research2), (c) delegate to generate.aisop.json for rebuild + ThreeDimTest on fixed version, (d) re-run simulate.aisop.json on fixed version (max 1 auto-fix retry to prevent infinite loop). If auto-fix reduces RED to 0, update pipeline_metadata with fixed results and proceed. If RED persists after retry, proceed with original simulation_report + auto_fix_attempted=true flag. SKIP CONDITIONS: simulate_red_count > 3 (too many for targeted fix), user explicitly declines auto-fix, or Create mode with no prior quality baseline. Pass simulation_report to ObservabilityStep.
SimulateStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. ENFORCEMENT per STANDARD.PL12: RECOMMENDED for Create, MANDATORY for Evolve (evolution introduces higher regression risk — derived reference errors and semantic inconsistencies are only detectable through simulation). Skip requires explicit pipeline_metadata.skip_reason. Auto-fix is advisory — user may decline and proceed with RED issues noted.
SimulateStep.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip simulation with WARNING, record in pipeline_metadata.skipped_stages -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

PostSimulateGate.step1: [ASSERT] SimulateStep executed. If false → go back to SimulateStep. | GATE(PL13): read simulate_quality_indicator. GREEN(0 red,<=2 yellow)→PASS→ObservabilityStep. Emit on_gate_pass if event_hooks configured. YELLOW(0 red,3+ yellow)→WARN. Per yellow→generate {issue_id,root_cause,affected_files,suggested_fix,fix_category:'MF'|'PL'|'simulation'|'by_design'|'other'}. YELLOW FIX: if yellow_fix_proposals non-empty→present per proposal {scenario_id,file_path,change_type,diff_preview,confidence,rationale}. USER OPTIONS(3): (a) 'auto_fix'(recommended)→apply HIGH confidence, review MEDIUM/LOW individually, (b) 'review_all'→manual per-issue, (c) 'accept_and_proceed'→proceed as-is. '--advanced'→fine-grained options. Approved→delegate to AutoFixEngine→re-run targeted Simulate(max 1 cycle). RED(any)→BLOCKED. QRG(quality_regression): baselines available→evaluate QRG1-5. QRG2/QRG3 RED→BLOCK. QRG1/QRG4 YELLOW→merge with simulate YELLOW count. TRUST: effective<declared→stricter YELLOW threshold(2+ instead of 3+). AUTO-DOWNGRADE: YELLOW 3+ AND not previously downgraded→effective_trust-1(min T1). Single downgrade per gate.
PostSimulateGate.step2: RED HANDLING: BLOCKED AND !auto_fix_attempted→user choice(PL19 choice): (a) 'auto_fix'→RED fix mini-pipeline, (b) 'proceed_with_risk'→PASS_WITH_RED, (c) 'rollback'→restore evolution_snapshot(PL26) or abort. After 1 auto-fix: only (b)+(c). RED FIX MINI-PIPELINE: global_retry_budget>0→ModifyStep(red_fix_directive)→Generate2(affected modules)→SimulateStep(failing categories). Skip QualityGate/Research3/ValidateStep. Max 1 cycle. RED persists→user manual decision.
PostSimulateGate.step3: ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'PostSimulateGate', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. QUALITY-SIMULATION COHERENCE CHECK: after determining simulate_quality_indicator, compare with QualityGate ThreeDimTest result. If ThreeDimTest grade >= S (score >= 4.50) but simulate_quality_indicator is YELLOW or RED, add coherence_note to pipeline_metadata: 'Quality-Simulation divergence detected. ThreeDimTest measures static code quality (structure, guards, consistency). Simulate measures runtime semantic behavior (scenario coverage, edge cases). These metrics are complementary, not contradictory. High static quality with simulation warnings indicates well-structured code with runtime edge cases to address.' COMPOSITE QUALITY (advisory): composite_score = threedimscore - (yellow_count * 0.02) - (red_count * 0.10). Report both threedimscore and composite_score in ReviewStep.
PostSimulateGate.constraints: Max 1 auto-fix retry triggered by this gate (separate from SimulateStep's own auto-fix). Gate does NOT re-run simulation itself — it delegates back to ModifyStep→Generate2→SimulateStep for the retry cycle. YELLOW issues are informational — never block on YELLOW. MF22 NON-WAIVABLE ENFORCEMENT (B3 v1.18.0): if any RED issue in simulate_quality_indicator has check_id='MF22' (safety gate signal enforcement), this RED is non-waivable — 'proceed_with_risk' option is NOT available. User must either auto_fix or rollback. Safety signal enforcement failures cannot be accepted. GATE RESULT PERSISTENCE (C4): after gate evaluation, write gate result snapshot to .pipeline_cache/{pipeline_run_id}/post_simulate_gate.json containing: { gate_result, simulate_quality_indicator, yellow_count, red_count, yellow_issues: [scenario_id], red_issues: [scenario_id], qrg_results, timestamp }. On pipeline resume: if post_simulate_gate.json exists and simulation files unchanged, skip re-evaluation and restore gate result from cache.
PostSimulateGate.Error: Gate evaluation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: treat as GREEN (proceed) with WARNING that gate was skipped -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ObservabilityStep.step1: [ASSERT] PostSimulateGate executed. If false → go back to PostSimulateGate. | INPUT VALIDATION: verify pipeline_metadata is non-null. If null → skip observability, log warning, pass empty telemetry_report to ReviewStep. Delegate to observability.aisop.json — pass pipeline_metadata (accumulated across all prior stages), version_history_dir ({workspace_dir}/{target_aisop_dir}/.version_history/).
ObservabilityStep.step2: Collect telemetry_report. Accumulate pipeline_metadata: { observability_bottleneck: telemetry_report.bottleneck_stage, observability_anomalies: telemetry_report.timing_anomalies.length, observability_score_trajectory: telemetry_report.score_trajectory }. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.current_stage='ObservabilityStep', increment completed_stages. CANCEL CHECK (PL21): if cancellation_requested, terminate gracefully. ATTESTATION EMIT: invoke attestation_token_emitter→{stage:'ObservabilityStep', status:'COMPLETE', evidence_hash, ts}→append attestation_chain. Pass telemetry_report to ReviewStep.
ObservabilityStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Non-blocking — observability failures do NOT block pipeline. CREATE MODE EXEMPTION: for Create pipeline (first-ever run, no version history), ObservabilityStep is automatically skipped — no historical data exists for comparison, timing baselines, or trend analysis. Skip reason recorded in pipeline_metadata.skipped_stages. For Evolve operations with >=2 prior versions, ObservabilityStep becomes RECOMMENDED.
ObservabilityStep.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip observability with WARNING -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ReviewStep.step1: [ASSERT] ObservabilityStep executed. If false → go back to ObservabilityStep. | Delegate to review.aisop.json — pass draft_path, test_report (from Generate2), traffic_report (from Generate2), validation_report (from ValidateStep), simulation_report (from SimulateStep), telemetry_report (from ObservabilityStep), old_version_context (from pipeline context), pipeline_metadata (accumulated), workspace_dir.
ReviewStep.step1_5: INTELLIGENT ROLLBACK DECISION ENGINE (B1 v1.6.0 — AIMultiple 2026 autonomous rollback governance; Basilisk provenance-based invariant verification; reinforcement learning pipeline self-healing): After PresentDraft delegation (step1) and before user refinement (step2), automatically evaluate whether this evolution should be rolled back based on accumulated pipeline evidence. PURPOSE: prevent deploying degraded evolutions by synthesizing quality signals from multiple pipeline stages into a single rollback recommendation — eliminating the need for users to manually correlate regression signals. METHOD: (1) QUALITY REGRESSION TRIGGER: load test_report from Generate2. If weighted_score delta (current vs PL26 snapshot baseline) < -0.15 → fire QRG_REGRESSION trigger with severity='high'. If delta < -0.05 → fire with severity='medium'. Delta >= -0.05 → no trigger. (2) SEMANTIC REGRESSION TRIGGER: load semantic_regression from test_report (produced by ThreeDimTest step4_8). If any drift item has severity='RED' (threshold drift or tool call change) → fire SEMANTIC_BREACH trigger with severity='high'. If only YELLOW drifts → fire with severity='low'. (3) FRAMEWORK COMPLETENESS TRIGGER: load framework_completeness from test_report (produced by ThreeDimTest step5_5). If framework_completeness_score < 0.5 → fire FRAMEWORK_DEGRADATION trigger with severity='medium'. If < 0.3 → severity='high'. (4) SEMANTIC TREND TRIGGER: load semantic_trend from test_report (produced by ThreeDimTest step4_9). If trend_health = 'critical' → fire TREND_CRITICAL trigger with severity='medium'. (5) DECISION MATRIX: count triggers by severity. high_count >= 2 → recommendation = 'suggest_rollback'. high_count == 1 → recommendation = 'review_carefully'. high_count == 0 AND medium_count >= 2 → recommendation = 'review_carefully'. Otherwise → recommendation = 'proceed'. (6) OUTPUT: rollback_assessment = { triggers_fired: [{ trigger_name, severity, detail }], high_count: int, medium_count: int, recommendation: 'proceed'|'review_carefully'|'suggest_rollback', rollback_path: string (PL26 snapshot directory path), risk_summary: string (1-sentence explanation) }. (7) USER GATE: if recommendation = 'suggest_rollback' → present to user: 'ROLLBACK SUGGESTED: {high_count} high-severity triggers fired. {risk_summary}. Rollback path: {rollback_path}. [Rollback] [Override and Continue] [Review Details]'. Rollback → invoke RollbackVersion with PL26 snapshot, pipeline terminates. Override → continue to step2 with WARNING in pipeline_metadata. review_carefully → display warning banner but do not block. proceed → silent pass. SKIP: Create mode (no baseline to compare against), or test_report unavailable (Generate2 failed).
ReviewStep.step2: On Finalize -> pipeline complete. QRG1 REGRESSION ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS: when ThreeDimTest score delta from previous version < -0.1, generate regression_analysis = {overall_delta: float, per_dimension_delta: {C: float, I: float, D: float}, top5_item_deltas: [{item_id, old_score, new_score, delta}], denominator_change: {old_item_count, new_item_count, new_items_added: [string]}, analysis_summary: string}. Include regression_analysis in pipeline_metadata and display in ReviewStep changelog under 'Quality Regression Analysis' section. PROGRESS (PL20): update pipeline_task.status='completed', set progress.percentage=100, emit final progress event. SIMULATION QUALITY ALERT: if simulate_quality_indicator in pipeline_metadata is YELLOW or RED, display prominent warning banner in final review: 'Simulation found {N} semantic issues — review before deployment.' List each YELLOW/RED scenario issue with description. Include simulate_quality_indicator in pipeline summary table. QRG REPORT: if pipeline_metadata.qrg_results is non-null, display QRG evaluation results (QRG1-QRG5) in final review summary. Highlight any YELLOW/RED QRG findings with specific regression details. EVENT EMISSION: if event_hooks configured, emit on_complete event: { pipeline_run_id, final_score: weighted_score, total_stages, elapsed_time, timestamp }. ATTESTATION CHAIN VERIFICATION: verify len(attestation_chain)==completed_stages, recompute chain hashes from chain_seed, emit attestation_integrity_report:{chain_length,expected,hash_valid:bool,broken_links:[]}. Broken chain→WARNING in final summary. EVOLUTION FITNESS AUTO-UPDATE (per S111): if self-evolution mode (auto_detection.is_self=true): (1) compute evolution_fitness per protocol_config.json evolution_fitness formula (density_trend×0.35 + volume_health×0.30 + compression_viability×0.20 + architecture_readiness×0.15), (2) determine fitness_trend from history_summary last 3 entries, (3) append current version entry to history_summary: ver:chars/growth%/logic/macro/micro/fitness, (4) write updated history_summary + fitness to protocol_config.json evolution_state. Auto-update eliminates manual fitness persistence — ReviewStep is single source of truth for fitness lifecycle.
ReviewStep.step2_5: EVOLUTION DIRECTION ALIGNMENT AUDIT (B2 v1.4.0 + B4 v1.5.0 STRUCTURED INPUT — AAAI/ACM AIES goal drift evaluation; Palisade Research specification gaming detection; PADL 2026 declarative constraint verification): For Evolve mode only. After pipeline completion and before final review presentation, verify that the evolution output ACTUALLY ALIGNS with the user's stated evolution direction. METHOD: (1) DIRECTION INPUT: PREFER structured evolution_directions from pipeline_context (produced by EvolveStep.step2 B4 v1.5.0 EVOLUTION DIRECTION STANDARDIZATION). If evolution_directions available (structured with category/priority fields) → use directly, set extraction_method='structured'. If NOT available (legacy pipeline or EvolveStep didn't produce structured output) → FALLBACK to NLP extraction from user's original evolution request text: parse direction keywords, extract English semantic equivalents for non-English text. Set extraction_method='nlp_fallback'. Log extraction_method in report. (2) CHANGE-DIRECTION MAPPING: for each B-change in evolution_plan.changes, check its direction_alignment field (set during EvolveStep tier presentation, B4 v1.5.0). If direction_alignment present → use directly. If absent (legacy) → scan change's step text, description, and research_sources for keyword overlap with direction keywords. Compute alignment_score per direction. (3) COVERAGE CHECK: for each direction, verify at least 1 B-change addresses it. Uncovered direction → YELLOW 'DIRECTION-UNCOVERED: evolution direction "{direction}" ({category}) has no corresponding B-change. User requested this but no change addresses it.' Priority-weighted: uncovered HIGH priority direction → escalate to RED. (4) IMBALANCE CHECK: if >60% of B-changes target a single direction while other directions have 0 coverage → YELLOW 'DIRECTION-IMBALANCE: {N}/{total} changes target "{dominant_direction}", {K} directions uncovered. Consider rebalancing.' (5) MEANS VERIFICATION: for each B-change, verify implementation approach is consistent with stated direction. Change claiming 'security hardening' should contain security logic, not cosmetic renaming. Mismatch → YELLOW 'DIRECTION-MISMATCH: B{id} claims to address "{direction}" but step content lacks relevant implementation.' (6) REPORT: direction_alignment: { extraction_method: 'structured'|'nlp_fallback', directions_requested: [{ keyword, category, priority }], directions_covered: [string], directions_uncovered: [string], changes_per_direction: { [direction]: [change_ids] }, alignment_score: float(0-1), imbalance_detected: boolean, means_verified: boolean }. Include in pipeline_metadata and display in ReviewStep final summary. SKIP: Create mode, or when no evolution directions identifiable.
ReviewStep.step3: AIAP.md UPDATE (single ownership — this step is the ONLY place AIAP.md is written/updated; review.aisop.json ChangelogGenerate prepares content but does NOT write AIAP.md): update AIAP.md version, modules list, quality fields via file_system. Verify AIAP.md version matches final .aisop.json version. Verify modules list matches actual files in directory. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename). Backup existing AIAP.md as .bak before overwrite. METADATA SYNC (P1 v3.4.0): After AIAP.md write, MUST synchronize: (1) agent_card.json — update version, description (reflect current features), tags (match AIAP.md tags). If agent_card.json missing→auto-generate per step4 MF17 logic. (2) quality_baseline.json — update version, pipeline, timestamp, evolution_from, evolution_notes.changes. (3) AIAP.md quality.weighted_score — MUST equal quality_baseline.weighted_score (single source of truth: quality_baseline computes, AIAP.md references). Mismatch→auto-correct AIAP.md to match quality_baseline value+log WARNING. GENERATOR SYNC (P5 v3.8.0): update agent_card.json safety_card.generator to 'aiap_creator_evolution_aiap v{creator_version}' where creator_version is read from Creator's own main.aisop.json version field. This ensures traceability: every evolution records which Creator version produced it. Record sync actions in pipeline_metadata.metadata_sync_report = { agent_card_updated, quality_baseline_updated, score_aligned, generator_synced }. BENCHMARK AUTO-SYNC (B6 v3.9.0): after quality_baseline sync, read quality_baseline.simulation totals (total_scenarios, pass_count). Update AIAP.md benchmark.simulation_coverage to 'v1.0.0-v{new_version}: {total_scenarios} scenarios' and benchmark.pass_rate to '{pass_count}/{total_scenarios} ({pct}%) — {red_count} RED, {yellow_count} YELLOW'. Source of truth: quality_baseline.simulation aggregated across all version entries. Log: 'BENCHMARK SYNC: simulation_coverage={N} scenarios, pass_rate={pct}%'. EVOLUTION DIRECTION PERSISTENCE (B4 v1.6.0 — StrategySoftware 2026 semantic reuse governed at scale; arXiv 2507.21046 inter-task knowledge consolidation; Meta AI NAACL 2024 goal drift longitudinal tracking): For Evolve mode only. After quality_baseline sync, persist evolution direction metadata for longitudinal tracking and cross-evolution learning. METHOD: (1) DIRECTION ARCHIVAL: read evolution_directions from pipeline_context (structured output from EvolveStep.step2 B4 v1.5.0). Write to quality_baseline.json evolution_history[current_version].directions = evolution_directions (preserving category, priority, keywords). If evolution_directions unavailable (legacy), extract from pipeline_metadata.user_request via NLP and store with extraction_method='nlp_reconstructed'. (2) DIRECTION CONCENTRATION TREND: load directions from last 3 evolution_history entries. Build direction_frequency_map: { category → count_across_versions }. If any single category appears in >60% of recent evolutions AND other categories have 0 appearances → emit DIRECTION-CONCENTRATION-ADVISORY: 'Last {N} evolutions focused exclusively on "{dominant_category}". Consider diversifying: [{underserved_categories}] may need attention.' Advisory-only (no score impact). Include in pipeline_metadata for ReviewStep display. (3) AIAP.md OPTIONAL FIELD: if AIAP.md contains a last_directions field (optional, not required), update with current evolution_directions summary (max 200 chars). If field absent, do NOT add it (backward compatible). (4) REPORT: direction_persistence = { archived: boolean, directions_count: int, concentration_trend: { dominant_category: string|null, advisory_emitted: boolean, distribution: { [category]: int } }, aiap_md_updated: boolean }. Log: 'DIRECTION PERSISTENCE: {N} directions archived to evolution_history, concentration={dominant|balanced}'.
ReviewStep.step4: POST-PIPELINE MANDATORY OUTPUT AUDIT (per MF17): Before computing governance_hash (PL14): (1) Enumerate mandatory output list per STANDARD MF17: AIAP.md, quality_baseline.json, agent_card.json (UNCONDITIONAL for all patterns), .version_history/{version}/meta.json. For Evolve mode additionally: .version_history/{version}/creation_experience.json. (2) For each mandatory output: verify file exists in target program directory via file_system. (3) Missing agent_card.json → attempt auto-generation from AIAP.md metadata: extract name, version, description, capabilities.offered, tools → generate minimal agent_card.json with safety_card stub (risk_level derived from trust_level). (3_5) QUALITY BASELINE AUTO-SCAFFOLDING (B4 v1.7.0 — SonarQube 'Clean as You Code' auto-baseline establishment; Jenkins Coverage Plugin baseline computation from current state; Qodana SARIF auto-scaffolding; JetBrains 2025 quality gate auto-generation): Missing quality_baseline.json → attempt auto-generation from pipeline data. PURPOSE: instead of blocking the pipeline when quality_baseline.json is absent (common for v1.0.0 programs or programs created before MF17 enforcement), auto-generate a valid baseline from the current pipeline's ThreeDimTest and SimulateStep results. METHOD: (a) EXTRACT SCORES: from test_report (produced by Generate2 ThreeDimTest): weighted_score, grade, C/I/D scores, MF check results, PL check results. From simulation_report (produced by SimulateStep): total_scenarios, passed, failed, pass_rate, category breakdown. (b) BUILD SCAFFOLD: quality_baseline = { scoring_scale: '1-5', version: current pipeline version, snapshot_date: current ISO date, three_dim_test: { weighted_score, grade, cognitive: { weight, score, notes }, intrinsic: { weight, score, na_items, notes }, detail: { weight, score, notes }, multi_file: MF summary string, pipeline: PL summary string }, simulation: { total_scenarios, passed, failed, pass_rate, categories: category_breakdown, result_summary: { GREEN, YELLOW, RED counts } }, regression_check: { QRG1: 'N/A — first baseline', QRG3: { score, floor: 3.0, result } }, evolution_notes: { ['v' + version + '_changes']: ['Initial quality baseline auto-generated by Creator pipeline (B4 v1.7.0)'] } }. (c) CREATE MODE HANDLING: for Create mode (no prior version), all fields populated from current pipeline results. No regression_check needed (first version). (d) EVOLVE MODE HANDLING: for Evolve mode, quality_baseline.json SHOULD exist (from PL26 snapshot or prior pipeline). If missing → WARNING 'quality_baseline.json missing in Evolve mode — auto-generating from current results. Prior version baselines unavailable for QRG regression check.' Generate scaffold but mark QRG1 as 'DEGRADED — no prior baseline available.' (e) WRITE: atomic write to target_program_path/quality_baseline.json. Log: 'MF17 AUTO-SCAFFOLD: quality_baseline.json generated from pipeline results (score: {score}/{grade}, {scenarios} scenarios).' (f) VERIFICATION: re-read generated file, parse JSON, verify version matches current. (4) Re-check after auto-generation. If ANY mandatory output still missing → BLOCK finalization: 'Missing mandatory output: {file_name}. Pipeline cannot compute governance_hash with incomplete outputs per MF17. Run pipeline stage {stage_name} to generate missing output.' (5) Record pipeline_metadata.mandatory_output_audit = { checked: [...], present: [...], auto_generated: [...], missing: [...], result: 'PASS' | 'BLOCK' }.
ReviewStep.step5: GOVERNANCE HASH (per STANDARD.PL14): compute SHA-256 of all .aisop.json AND .aisip.json file contents in target directory, concatenated in alphabetical filename order. DUAL MODE (B1 v1.11.0): when target_format='DUAL' or .aisip.json files exist in target directory, include both format files in hash computation: sorted(*.aisop.json + *.aisip.json). This ensures governance integrity covers all program representations. Write computed hash to AIAP.md governance_hash field. GOVERNANCE HASH VERIFICATION: after computing SHA-256 governance_hash and writing it to AIAP.md, perform verification: re-read all .aisop.json files, recompute SHA-256, compare with written value. If mismatch → WARN 'governance_hash verification failed — possible concurrent file modification' and retry hash computation once. If retry also mismatches → flag in pipeline_metadata.hash_verification_failed=true.
ReviewStep.step6: ROOT QUALITY_BASELINE UPDATE: in addition to writing .version_history/{version}/quality_baseline.json, also write/overwrite the root quality_baseline.json with the latest version's scores. This root file is the active baseline used by future pipeline runs for regression detection (QRG1). Content: same as version snapshot but with version field updated to current. CREATION EXPERIENCE (per STANDARD.data_contracts.creation_experience): compile pipeline learning record from accumulated pipeline_metadata: (a) challenges — extract stages with retries, fallbacks, or QualityGate failures from pipeline_metadata, (b) quality_outcome — weighted_score + grade + dimension_scores from test_report, (c) simulation_highlights — coverage + dead_nodes + red_issues from simulation_report, (d) key_decisions — LEVEL_A accept/reject decisions from EvolveStep, (e) research_insights — top 3 verified_findings by relevance from research_context. Persist to .version_history/{version}/creation_experience.json. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename). Record pipeline_metadata.creation_experience_written = true. This is the final step before pipeline completion.
ReviewStep.step7: DESCRIPTION REFRESH(C3): LEVEL_B changes→verify (1) AIAP.md summary mentions new capabilities(missing→YELLOW), (2) modified modules' summary/description reflect changes(unchanged→YELLOW), (3) AIAP_cn.md exists→advisory for localization update. Advisory-only. MODULE METADATA SYNC: per module re-read .aisop.json→verify nodes count, side_effects vs tools, idempotent vs behavior(file_system write→false unless justified), critical flag. Auto-correct mismatches+log corrections. MF29 VERSION SYNC (P1 v3.4.0): verify version equality across all metadata files: AIAP.md.version == main.aisop.json.version == agent_card.json.version == quality_baseline.json.version. Any mismatch→RED: 'MF29 FAIL: version mismatch {file}={ver}, expected {expected}'. Auto-correct all to AIAP.md version. MF30 SCORE CONSISTENCY (P2 v3.4.0): verify AIAP.md.quality.weighted_score == quality_baseline.three_dim_test.weighted_score. Mismatch→YELLOW: 'MF30: score inconsistency AIAP.md={x} vs baseline={y}'. Auto-correct AIAP.md to baseline value (baseline is authoritative). TAGS LIMIT AUDIT (P3 v3.4.0): if AIAP.md tags count>30→AUTO-FIX: remove internal implementation tags (step-compression,dry-constraints,inline-trace,semantic-compression,node-merge,shared-constraints), merge synonyms, keep user-facing terms, cap at 30. Auto-sync agent_card.json tags to match. Log: 'TAGS AUTO-FIX: {old}→{new} ({removed} compressed)'. KEYWORDS LIMIT AUDIT (P7 v3.5.0): if AIAP.md discovery_keywords count>20→AUTO-FIX: remove entries duplicated in tags[], remove internal terms, cap at 20. Log: 'KEYWORDS AUTO-FIX: {old}→{new} ({deduped} removed)'. MF31 MERMAID AUDIT (P4 v3.5.0): per ValidateStep MF31. AUTO-FIX: (a) function exists but not in Mermaid→add node to Mermaid graph at correct topological position with edges to/from adjacent nodes. (b) Mermaid rectangle node has no function→remove from Mermaid graph, reconnect edges. (c) PascalCase diamond suggesting independent node→rename to lowercase decision label (e.g., TypeGate{leaf/branch?}→type?{leaf/branch?} or merge into parent output edges). MF31c ENDNODE NAMING (P6 v3.8.0, B5 v3.9.0): iterate over ALL aisop.* keys in each module (not just aisop.main — includes aisop.intent_dispatch, aisop.sub_*, etc.). For each sub-graph: scan Mermaid for terminal nodes. If terminal node uses 'End', 'end', 'Finish', or any variant other than 'endNode((End))'→AUTO-FIX: replace with 'endNode((End))' in Mermaid graph. Log: 'MF31c: {sub_graph}.terminal {old}→endNode((End)) normalized.' Log: 'MF31 AUTO-FIX: {added} nodes added, {removed} stale nodes removed, {renamed} diamonds renamed, {endnode_fixed} endNode normalized.'
ReviewStep.step8: FINAL ADJUSTED SCORE (INS-005): After ReviewStep.Finalize updates AIAP.md, quality_baseline.json, and agent_card.json to v{new_version}, re-evaluate D6 (version sync), D8 (agent card completeness), and D10 (quality baseline sync) using the NOW-CURRENT files. Compute final_adjusted_score = C_avg × 0.25 + I_avg × 0.45 + D_adjusted_avg × 0.30 where D_adjusted_avg replaces the Stage 8 D6/D8/D10 scores with the post-Finalize re-scored values. Record in pipeline_metadata: { stage8_score: {original}, final_adjusted_score: {adjusted}, adjustment_delta: {delta} }. Report both scores to user: 'Stage 8 score: {X}, Final adjusted score: {Y} (D6/D8/D10 re-scored after ReviewStep file updates)'. If final_adjusted_score < stage8_score, emit WARNING — this should never happen (ReviewStep only adds correct data).
ReviewStep.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Review has full context from all pipeline stages.
ReviewStep.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present draft directly -> inform. SEVERITY: CRITICAL (per PL27) — max 3 retries then abort.

ModifyPipeline.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION + INIT: resolve target AISOP file (same logic as PipelineStart Evolve mode). Initialize pipeline_metadata = { pipeline_run_id: UUID_v4, pipeline_type: 'modify' }.
ModifyPipeline.step2: EXECUTE MODIFY PIPELINE SEQUENCE: delegate each stage sequentially, accumulating pipeline_metadata throughout: (a) Research(quality) — research.aisop.json with research_mode='quality', (b) Modify — modify.aisop.json with target_file + research_context, (c) Generate — generate.aisop.json for rebuild + ThreeDimTest, (d) Validate — inline structural + integrity + quality checks per STANDARD validation checklist, (e) Simulate (RECOMMENDED) — simulate.aisop.json if not skipped (record skip_reason if skipped), (f) PostSimulateGate (RECOMMENDED, after Simulate) — evaluate simulate_quality_indicator per STANDARD.PL13. GREEN: proceed. YELLOW: warn + proceed. RED: offer auto-fix or user override per PostSimulateGate logic. Skip if Simulate was skipped. (g) Review — review.aisop.json with all accumulated reports.
ModifyPipeline.step3: FINALIZE: on ReviewStep completion, pipeline complete. Apply same AIAP.md update and governance_hash logic as full pipeline ReviewStep.step3.
ModifyPipeline.constraints: Shorter pipeline: Research(quality)→Modify→Generate→Validate→[Simulate]→[PostSimulateGate]→Review. No Evolve step. No Research(structure/compliance). Version bump follows STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management: modify_only→patch+1.
ModifyPipeline.Error: Any step fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present partial results -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

Validate.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | STANDALONE MODE: Read .aisop.json via file_system. Reject >500KB or nesting >20.
Validate.step2: STRUCTURAL (1-6): protocol/id/name/version/summary/description, tools array, parameters, system_prompt, instruction, aisop.main Mermaid, functions.
Validate.step3: INTEGRITY (7-10): matching Mermaid<->functions, Error fields present, endNode reachable, no orphan edges.
Validate.step4: QUALITY (11-18): C2 node load, C4 data flow, I1 tool honesty, I2 security guards (with zero-tool adaptation per STANDARD I2), I4 circuit-breaker pattern, I6 resilience + semantic accuracy, D4 consistency, D5 boundary rules. NOTE: for comprehensive quality testing including all C/I/D dimensions, use full pipeline ThreeDimTest.
Validate.step5: Suggest: (1) run full pipeline (Create/Evolve) for comprehensive improvement, (2) run Modify pipeline for targeted fixes.
Validate.constraints: Read-only. No domain research in standalone mode. Show fixes, not just failures.
Validate.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: specific error + minimal fix suggestion -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

SimulateFlow.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | STANDALONE MODE: Delegate to simulate.aisop.json — pass target, mode, workspace_dir.
SimulateFlow.step2: Collect simulation_report. Suggest: (1) run full pipeline for improvement, (2) run Modify pipeline for fixes.
SimulateFlow.constraints: Read-only. ALWAYS delegate. No domain research in standalone mode.
SimulateFlow.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: static Mermaid path trace -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

Compare.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: accept two target paths (AISOP files or directories). If only one provided, prompt for the second. Resolve each path using PipelineStart logic (exact path, name scan, numbered list). Support: (a) two versions of same AISOP (e.g., v1.0.0.bak vs current), (b) two different AISOPs.
Compare.step2: Read both AISOP files via file_system. For multi-file (Pattern B+), read all sub-modules. Extract: Pattern, version, node count, tools[], parameters, NLU intents (if any), function names, constraint patterns. MULTI-FILE DIFF: for Pattern B+ programs (multiple .aisop.json files), generate per-module diff matrix: | Module | AISOP A Nodes | AISOP B Nodes | Delta | Functions Added | Functions Removed | Functions Modified | For each modified function: list changed steps with brief diff summary.
Compare.step3: Generate structured diff report: | Aspect | AISOP A | AISOP B | Delta |. Aspects: Pattern, version, functional node count, tools, parameters, NLU intents, function count, I2 guard coverage, weighted_score (if available from .version_history/). Highlight additions, removals, and changes. CROSS-MODULE IMPACT: for multi-file diffs, analyze cross-module data flow changes: (1) New data contracts (parameters added to cross-module calls), (2) Removed data contracts (parameters no longer passed), (3) Modified data contracts (parameter type/format changes). Present as: | Data Flow | AISOP A | AISOP B | Impact |
Compare.step4: Suggest: (1) evolve the older version, (2) modify specific differences, (3) validate either file.
Compare.constraints: Read-only. No modifications. Both files must be within workspace_dir. (D) Path guard — reject '..'.
Compare.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: compare metadata only (without full function diff) -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

Explain.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | Explain AISOP concepts with concrete JSON examples. Topics: AISOP structure, Mermaid flow, functions, constraints, Error handling, fractal patterns (A-F), quality dimensions (C/I/D), Pipeline architecture.
Explain.step2: Suggest: create AISOP (full pipeline), modify existing (modify pipeline), validate structure (standalone).
Explain.Error: Concept not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: use built-in examples with general explanation -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

General.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | Explain capabilities: (1) Create — full pipeline from scratch, (2) Evolve — full pipeline with structural evolution, (3) Modify — quality improvement pipeline, (4) Validate — standalone structural check, (5) Simulate — standalone coverage analysis, (6) Compare — side-by-side version comparison, (7) Explain — concepts, (8) Discover — search existing programs, (9) Deprecate — lifecycle management, (10) Export — AIAP→SKILL.md, (11) Import — SKILL.md→AIAP, (12) Package — pack/unpack .aiap archives, (13) Convert — AISOP↔AISIP format conversion. Lead with action suggestions.
General.step2: Suggest: create AISOP, modify existing, validate structure, discover existing programs.
General.Error: Intent unclear -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present numbered capability list -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

DiscoverStep.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | LOCAL SCAN: workspace_dir *_aiap/→read AIAP.md frontmatter(name,version,status,summary,triggers,examples,keywords,pattern). Filter archived(show separately). IDENTITY(I11): active programs→verify identity block(program_id format,publisher,verified_on). Missing→IDENTITY_UNVERIFIED. REMOTE(PL22): registry_uri non-null→GET search?q={query}&tags={keywords}. Auth: AIAP_REGISTRY_TOKEN Bearer. 401/403 without token→degrade local+WARNING. Timeout 3s. Success→merge(local priority), mark REMOTE_UNVERIFIED. Error→local only+WARNING. Cache TTL=3600s.
DiscoverStep.step2: MATCH: compare query vs each program's (a) discovery_keywords(intersection), (b) intent_examples(semantic similarity), (c) triggers(pattern match), (d) summary(domain relevance). Compute relevance_score(0-1), rank descending. Label LOCAL/REMOTE. ENDPOINTS: parse mcp→'MCP-capable', a2a→'A2A-capable'. Query mentions 'MCP/tool/API/endpoint'→boost endpoint programs. LAYER 2 STRUCTURED SEARCH: build index from extended frontmatter. Facets: by_tags, by_capabilities, by_trust_level, by_pattern, by_security_scheme. Supplement semantic matching. Include facets summary {tags:{count}, capabilities:{count}, trust_levels:{count}}.
DiscoverStep.step3: PRESENT: numbered list with name, version, status, summary, relevance_score, match_reason. If top score > 0.8: suggest 'Consider Evolve/Modify instead of Create.' If no matches: suggest 'No matching programs. Consider Create.' For REMOTE_UNVERIFIED results, offer additional actions: (a) fetch and install locally, (b) view detailed metadata, (c) verify governance_hash before operating.
DiscoverStep.step4: FOLLOW-UP: (1) Evolve matched, (2) Modify matched, (3) Create new anyway, (4) Compare two matches, (5) Refine search.
DiscoverStep.constraints: Read-only. Scan limited to workspace_dir. (D) Path guard — reject '..'. Max 50 programs scanned. Remote query requires registry_uri parameter. Remote timeout: 3 seconds. Max remote results: 20.
DiscoverStep.Error: Scan fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list directories without parsing -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

DeprecateFlow.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: resolve target AIAP program. Read AIAP.md status. VALIDATION: archived → reject; draft → suggest delete; deprecated → offer archive; active → proceed.
DeprecateFlow.step2: DEPRECATION (active→deprecated): collect reason. Set AIAP.md: status='deprecated', deprecated_date=today, successor=user-specified-or-null, deprecation_reason=reason. Scan workspace for dependents — if found, display WARNING.
DeprecateFlow.step3: MIGRATION GUIDE: generate migration_guide.md: deprecated program info, reason, successor mapping, data migration notes, 90-day timeline. WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename).
DeprecateFlow.step4: ARCHIVE (deprecated→archived): verify 90-day window elapsed (or user force-confirms). Set status='archived'. Update governance_hash. No further Evolve/Modify allowed (D9 enforcement).
DeprecateFlow.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: atomic write (.tmp then rename). Backup AIAP.md as .bak. Valid transitions: active→deprecated, deprecated→archived only.
DeprecateFlow.Error: Resolution fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list available programs -> inform. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.

ExportSKILL.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: resolve target AIAP program. Verify AIAP.md exists and status != 'archived'.
ExportSKILL.step2: Delegate to advisor.aisop.json — advisor_type='skill_export', target_path, workspace_dir. MCP ADAPTER: for MCP Server wrapper generation, delegate to advisor.aisop.json with advisor_type='mcp_adapter' (available via generate.aisop.json for new programs, or direct user request 'generate MCP wrapper for X').
ExportSKILL.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Target must be valid AIAP program.
ExportSKILL.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list available programs -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ImportSKILL.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: resolve SKILL.md file path. Verify file exists and readable.
ImportSKILL.step2: Delegate to advisor.aisop.json — advisor_type='skill_import', target_path, workspace_dir.
ImportSKILL.constraints: ALWAYS delegate. Target must be valid SKILL.md file.
ImportSKILL.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: request valid path -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

PackageFlow.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: determine pack or unpack mode from user request. Pack: resolve target AIAP program directory path. Unpack: resolve .aiap file path within workspace_dir.
PackageFlow.step2: Delegate to advisor.aisop.json — advisor_type='package', pass target_path and workspace_dir.
PackageFlow.constraints: ALWAYS delegate to advisor. (D) Path guard — target must resolve within workspace_dir.
PackageFlow.Error: Not found -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list available AIAP programs or .aiap files in workspace -> inform. SEVERITY: OPTIONAL (per PL27) — max 1 retry then skip.

ConvertIntent.step1: [ASSERT] NLU executed. If false → go back to NLU. | FILE RESOLUTION: resolve source AIAP program directory path from user request. Detect target store from context or user specification. Default target stores: AISOP source → aisip_aiap_store/, AISIP source → aisop_aiap_store/.
ConvertIntent.step2: Delegate to convert.aisop.json — pass source_path, target_store, direction (if user specified). Convert module handles: auto-direction detection, §4 topology conversion (Mermaid↔JSON flow dict), functions byte-identical copy, governance file alignment, migration manifest generation.
ConvertIntent.step3: After convert module returns, prompt user to run verification: (1) Creator validate on target program, (2) Creator simulate on target program, (3) DUAL-C1/D1/D2 consistency check against _source files, (4) Optional round-trip test.
ConvertIntent.constraints: ALWAYS delegate to convert module. Source program is READ-ONLY. (D) Path guard — paths within workspace_dir only. Bidirectional: AISOP→AISIP and AISIP→AISOP supported.
ConvertIntent.Error: Source not found → list available programs. Convert fails → report error from convert module. SEVERITY: IMPORTANT (per PL27) — max 2 retries then offer degraded continuation.
